text: Lesson 37
zh: 第37课

text: The process of ageing
zh: 衰老的过程

text: What is one of the most unpleasant discoveries we make about ourselves as we get older?
zh: 随着年龄增长，我们对自己所发现的最不愉快的事情之一是什么？

text: At the age of twelve years, the human body is at its most vigorous.
zh: 在十二岁时，人体处于最旺盛的状态。

text: It has yet to reach its full size and strength, and its owner his or her full intelligence: but at this age the likelihood of death is least.
zh: 它还没有达到最大的体型和力量，其主人也还没有达到智力的巅峰；但在这个年龄，死亡的可能性最低。

text: Earlier, we were infants and young children, and consequently more vulnerable;
zh: 更早的时候，我们是婴儿和幼童，因此更加脆弱；

text: later, we shall undergo a progressive loss of our vigour and resistance which, though imperceptible at first
zh: 而在此之后，我们的活力和抵抗力将逐渐衰退，起初这种衰退难以察觉，

text: will finally become so steep that we can live no longer,
zh: 但最终会变得如此急剧，以至于我们无法继续生存下去，

text: however well we look after ourselves, and however well society, and our doctors, look after us.
zh: 无论我们多么注意保养自己，也无论社会和医生多么悉心照顾我们。

text: This decline in vigour with the passing of time is called ageing.
zh: 这种随时间推移而出现的活力衰退被称为衰老。

text: It is one of the most unpleasant discoveries which we all make that we must decline in this way, that if we escape wars,
zh: 这是我们所有人都会发现的最不愉快的事情之一：我们必然会以这种方式衰退，即使躲过了战争、

text: accidents and diseases we shall eventually 'die of old age', and that this happens at a rate which differs little from person to person,
zh: 事故和疾病，我们最终也会'寿终正寝'，而且这一过程在不同人身上发生的速率相差不大，

text: so that there are heavy odds in favour of our dying between the ages of 65 and 80.
zh: 以至于我们在65岁到80岁之间死亡的可能性非常大。

text: Some of us will die sooner, a few will live longer -- on into a ninth or tenth decade.
zh: 有些人会更早去世，少数人会活得更久——甚至活到八九十岁乃至上百岁。

text: But the chances are against it,
zh: 但这种可能性很小，

text: and there is a virtual limit on how long we can hope to remain alive, however lucky and robust we are.
zh: 无论我们多么幸运、多么强壮，我们能存活的时间实际上都是有限的。

text: Normal people tend to forget this process unless and until they are reminded of it.
zh: 正常人往往会忘记这一过程，除非有什么提醒他们。

text: We are so familiar with the fact that man ages, that people have for years assumed that the process of losing vigour with time,
zh: 我们对人会衰老这一事实太熟悉了，以至于多年来人们一直认为，随着时间推移而丧失活力、

text: of becoming more likely to die the older we get, was something self-evident
zh: 随着年龄增长死亡可能性增加，这是不言而喻的，

text: like the cooling of a hot kettle or the wearing-out of a pair of shoes.
zh: 就像热水壶会变凉，鞋子会磨损一样。

text: They have also assumed that all animals,
zh: 人们也认为所有动物、

text: and probably other organisms such as trees, or even the universe itself, must in the nature of things 'wear out'.
zh: 甚至树木等其他生物，乃至宇宙本身，按事物本性都必然会'耗损殆尽'。

text: Most animals we commonly observe do in fact age as we do,
zh: 我们通常观察到的大多数动物，事实上确实会像我们一样衰老，

text: if given the chance to live long enough;
zh: 只要它们有机会活得足够长；

text: and mechanical systems like a wound watch, or the sun,
zh: 而像上了发条的手表或太阳这样的机械系统，

text: do in fact run out of energy in accordance with the second law of thermodynamics (whether the whole universe does so is a moot point at present).
zh: 确实会依照热力学第二定律耗尽能量（整个宇宙是否也是如此，目前尚有争议）。

text: But these are not analogous to what happens when man ages.
zh: 但这些都不能类比人类衰老的情形。

text: A run-down watch is still a watch and can be rewound.
zh: 一块停走的表仍然是一块表，可以重新上发条。

text: An old watch, by contrast, becomes so worn and unreliable that it eventually is not worth mending.
zh: 相比之下，一块老旧的表会变得磨损严重、不再可靠，最终甚至不值得修理。

text: But a watch could never repair itself--it does not consist of living parts, only of metal, which wears away by friction.
zh: 但手表永远无法自我修复——它并非由活的部件组成，只是金属，会因摩擦而磨损。

text: We could, at one time, repair ourselves--well enough, at least, to overcome all but the most instantly fatal illnesses and accidents.
zh: 我们曾经能够自我修复——至少足以战胜除最致命的疾病和事故之外的一切。

text: Between twelve and eighty years we gradually lose this power;
zh: 在十二岁到八十岁之间，我们逐渐失去了这种自愈能力；

text: an illness which at 12 would knock us over, at 80 can knock us out, and into our grave.
zh: 一场在12岁时只会让我们倒下的疾病，在80岁时却可能让我们一蹶不振，甚至送命。

text: If we could stay as vigorous as we are at twelve,
zh: 如果我们能一直保持十二岁时那样的活力，

text: it would take about 700 years for half of us to die, and another 700 for the survivors to be reduced by half again.
zh: 那么大约需要700年，我们中一半的人才会去世，再过700年，幸存者中又有一半人会去世。
